channel.title: Aviation on Carlos A. Planchón
channel.description: Recent content in Aviation on Carlos A. Planchón

item.title: Aerodynamics: Lift and Drag
item.description:
Aerodynamics studies how air flows around bodies and the forces generated. For aircraft, the two primary aerodynamic forces are lift and drag.

Lift

Lift is the force perpendicular to the relative wind, counteracting weight. It is generated by the wing’s airfoil shape and the principle of Bernoulli:

L = ½ × ρ × V² × S × Cₗ

Where ρ is air density, V is velocity, S is wing area, and Cₗ is the lift coefficient.

item.title: VFR Decision-Making with Scattered Cloud Cover
item.description:
Meteorology is not a static snapshot; it is a fluid system in constant evolution. When you fly VFR (Visual Flight Rules), your primary survival metric is visibility and cloud separation.

On a recent flight, conditions reported scattered clouds at 3,000 feet.

The Pilot’s Mental Model vs. the Engineer’s

As an engineer, I like deterministic data. As a pilot, you have to operate with probabilities and error margins. The Pipistrel Alpha Trainer’s POH gives you exact performance numbers, but the atmosphere didn’t read the manual.
